summary: First-pass frontend-only testing of Altios Labs marketing site (static, no backend/auth). Homepage, bento grid, service pages, FAQ accordion, mobile nav/Sheet, dropdown, invalid-slug redirect, and Get in Touch all verified working. Found one real bug: Process/About/Contact nav links do not scroll to the target section (anchor scroll is broken both cross-page and same-page).

integration_issues.issue: Clicking 'Process', 'About', or 'Contact' nav links (react-router Link to='/#process' etc.) updates the URL hash (e.g. url becomes '/#process') but does NOT scroll the viewport to the target section — window.scrollY stays 0. Reproduced both when already on homepage and when navigating from a service page (/services/websites -> Process link). React Router's client-side navigation does not trigger native browser hash scrolling, and there's no useEffect/hash-scroll handler implemented to compensate.

action_items: Fix anchor scrolling for Process/About/Contact nav links: add a scroll-to-hash handler (e.g. in Layout.jsx using useLocation + useEffect that calls document.getElementById(hash).scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'}) whenever location.hash changes, including on initial mount when navigating from another route) so both same-page and cross-page navigation to /#process, /#why-us, /#contact actually scroll into view.
Verify same fix also covers Footer's '/#why-us' (About) and '/#contact' (Contact) links, and mobile Sheet nav links, since they share the same Link-based pattern.
critical_code_review_comments: siteData.js is a well-structured single source of truth for content — good separation of copy from markup.
VisualPlaceholder.jsx correctly implements the intentional dashed-border placeholder per user request — not a bug.
ServicePage.jsx correctly uses Navigate to redirect invalid slugs to home — confirmed working.
Data-testid coverage is thorough and consistent across Navbar, Footer, BentoGrid, ServiceFAQ, GetInTouch as specified in files_of_reference.

Root-cause of anchor scroll bug: NAV_LINKS hrefs are of form '/#process' rendered via react-router <Link>, which performs SPA navigation (pushes history state) but does not invoke the browser's native hash-scroll behavior; no equivalent handler exists in Layout.jsx to compensate.

success_rate.backend: N/A - not tested per request (static site, no backend)

test_credentials: None required - no auth on this app
seed_data_creation: None - static content site, no data creation needed
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified: homepage hero + CTAs, Book a Call opens cal.com/souravdash in new tab, Services dropdown (desktop) navigates correctly, bento grid renders 3 large + 2 small tiles in correct DOM order on both desktop and mobile (390px), mobile hamburger Sheet opens/lists all links + closes on nav, FAQ accordion expands/collapses on service pages, GetInTouch section + mailto link works on both homepage and service pages, footer renders correctly with all columns, invalid slug (/services/doesnotexist) correctly redirects to '/'. Only unresolved issue: Process/About/Contact anchor-scroll bug documented above — needs a hash-scroll effect in Layout.jsx or similar. No dark-on-dark contrast issues observed in Hero, WhyUs, Process, Testimonials, Footer, GetInTouch screenshots reviewed.
rca of the issue: React Router <Link to='/#section'> only updates URL/history; it does not trigger the browser's default same-page hash scroll-into-view because SPA client-side routing intercepts navigation. No custom scroll-restoration/hash-scroll logic was added in Layout.jsx to listen for location.hash changes and call scrollIntoView. Fix: add a small effect (e.g. in Layout or a ScrollToHash helper component) that runs on every location change and, if location.hash is present, scrolls the matching element into view (with a short delay/retry if the target page needs to mount first when coming from another route).
